final-capstone-file-room-handoff.label: Final Capstone: Community File Room Handoff
final-capstone-file-room-handoff.summary: Clean a small file room workspace with the full beginner terminal toolkit.

final-capstone-file-room-handoff-termina.title: Inspect and create the handoff area
final-capstone-file-room-handoff-termina.hint: Use the terminal. Check your work with `pwd`, `ls`, `cat`, or `find` before clicking Run.

help.concept: This task checks the workspace you build, not one perfect command line.
help.hint_1: Create folders before putting files inside them.
help.hint_2: When you are unsure, inspect the folder first, then make the next small change.

final-capstone-file-room-handoff-termina.starterCode: # Use the terminal for this project step.

0.message: Use `pwd` to confirm where you are.

2.message: Create the handoff folders with `mkdir` or `mkdir -p`.

0.message: Do not use sudo in this beginner workspace.

1.message: Do not use recursive rm in this course.

2.message: Permissions commands are not needed for this workspace.

3.message: Do not download anything for this exercise.

final-capstone-file-room-handoff-termina.title: Write the handoff README
final-capstone-file-room-handoff-termina.prompt: Write `Community file room handoff` to `handoff/README.txt`, then append `Agenda, guest list, and venue notes prepared`. Read it with `cat`.
final-capstone-file-room-handoff-termina.hint: Use the terminal. Check your work with `pwd`, `ls`, `cat`, or `find` before clicking Run.

help.concept: This task checks the workspace you build, not one perfect command line.

help.hint_2: When you are unsure, inspect the folder first, then make the next small change.

final-capstone-file-room-handoff-termina.starterCode: # Use the terminal for this project step.

0.message: Write the first README line with `>`.

1.message: Append the second README line with `>>`.

0.message: Do not use sudo in this beginner workspace.

1.message: Do not use recursive rm in this course.

2.message: Permissions commands are not needed for this workspace.

3.message: Do not download anything for this exercise.

final-capstone-file-room-handoff-termina.title: Back up the guest list and remove scratch
final-capstone-file-room-handoff-termina.prompt: Copy the guest list into `backup/guest-list-backup.txt`. Remove only `tmp-scratch.txt`, then list `inbox`.
final-capstone-file-room-handoff-termina.hint: Use the terminal. Check your work with `pwd`, `ls`, `cat`, or `find` before clicking Run.

final-capstone-file-room-handoff-termina.title: Verify and mark ready
final-capstone-file-room-handoff-termina.prompt: Count the guest list lines with `wc -l`, append `Ready for pickup` to the README, and create `handoff/READY.txt`.

capstone-synopsis.title: Community file room handoff
capstone-synopsis.bodyMarkdown:
The community center has a messy file room before an event. Your job is to inspect first, build a clean handoff area, write a README, move files, back up the guest list, safely remove a scratch file, and mark the folder ready.

This capstone uses the whole course: `pwd`, `ls`, `cd`, `mkdir`, `touch`, `echo`, `>`, `>>`, `cat`, `wc -l`, `cp`, `mv`, and safe `rm`.
